factors.name: Geopolitical Risk-Off (Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire)
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreed Wednesday reduced acute Middle East premium; oil prices fell 3% on Trump reluctance to restart Iran war. Safe-haven demand typically deflates when geopolitical tensions ease, weighing on gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness (Risk Appetite Recovery)
factors.summary: EUR/USD edged higher to 1.1625, GBP/USD bounced to 1.3439, and DXY likely softened on ceasefire relief. Weaker USD is directly bullish for gold; this is the dominant driver of recent XAU/USD strength (+0.32% in 4h, +1.40% earlier per FXStreet 14:15 report).

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Rhetoric (Schmid, Daly)
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid (hawkish) reiterated inflation remains top priority; Daly stated 'returning inflation to target remains top priority.' While policy is described as 'in a good place,' hawkish tilt implies higher-for-longer rates, which suppresses gold via higher real yields.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS) — Proxy
factors.summary: No direct TIPS data in this release window, but nominal 10y Treasury yields under pressure from ceasefire relief and softer geopolitical risk. Real yields likely mixed; without a fresh CPI surprise, real yields may not have moved materially lower — limiting upside for gold.

factors.name: Treasury Market Stress (BOE Bailey Commentary)
factors.summary: BoE Gov. Bailey noted markets 'orderly but stressed at times,' citing debt market leverage concerns. UK 10y rose 97bp (Feb 26 to May 18), US 10y rose 76bp. This backdrop suggests ongoing term-premium pressure but does not directly move gold short-term.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Risk Sentiment Proxy)
factors.summary: Oil snapped 3-day win streak on ceasefire and Trump reluctance to escalate; WTI/Brent fell ~3% per news. Lower oil typically softens near-term inflation expectations, reducing gold's inflation hedge appeal and favoring the USD.

factors.name: BoJ Rate Hike Expectations (JPY Strength)
factors.summary: Market prices in BoJ hike at June meeting; USD/JPY trading near 159.90 with yen firming. JPY strength competes with gold as safe-haven; carry-unwind risk if BoJ tightens further, reducing carry appetite for speculative longs including gold.

factors.name: Upcoming US Jobs Report (Tomorrow, June 5)
factors.summary: Treasury Sec. Bessent hinted he wishes jobs data had come out today, raising insider-knowledge concerns. NFP tomorrow is a major catalyst; hot print = BEARISH gold (Fed holds higher longer), cool print = BULLISH gold (pivot hopes). Market is pre-positioned for volatility.

factors.name: Equity Market Momentum (Dow +1.84%, broad rally)
factors.summary: Dow surged to record 51,000+ on risk-on rotation from geopolitical easing and energy relief. Broad equity strength typically correlates with weaker safe-haven demand for gold; when equities rally on lower tail risk, gold underperforms.

factors.name: Inflation Narrative (Recent Stubbornness)
factors.summary: Fed commentary remains firmly hawkish on inflation being 'too high' and a '5-year fight.' No fresh CPI/PCE surprise in this window, so inflation expectations likely range-bound. Without new downside surprise, real yields unlikely to compress further, capping gold upside.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied ~1.4% earlier in the session ($4,497 intraday) on USD weakness driven by Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and reduced geopolitical tail-risk premium. Current price $4,507 reflects a partial retention of those gains (+0.32% in 4h). However, the ceasefire also triggered a broad risk-on move: equities surged to record highs, oil fell 3%, and safe-haven demand deflated. Fed hawkish commentary (Schmid, Daly) continues to anchor real yields, limiting upside. The bounce is in 'Phase 2'—move is underway but faces resistance from competing forces (equity strength, oil weakness, rate premium).
catalysts_ahead: US Non-Farm Payroll (NFP) – Friday June 5, 8:30 ET – CRITICAL; hot print BEARISH gold, cool print BULLISH
BoJ Policy Decision – mid-June; rate hike would tighten carry and weigh on gold
Michigan Consumer Sentiment – Friday June 6; weak data BULLISH gold, strong data BEARISH
Middle East escalation risk – Hezbollah rejected ceasefire per news; any renewal of hostilities BULLISH geopolitical premium
Fed speakers – any further hawkish guidance BEARISH gold
screening.risk_warning: If NFP print beats expectations decisively (strong employment, wage growth), expect sharp gold sell-off and USD strength (DXY +0.5–1%), invalidating the buy bias. Alternatively, if Hezbollah or Iran re-escalates, geopolitical premium re-inflates and gold re-tests $4,520+. Current move is highly sensitive to tomorrow's jobs data.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway but not extended). Wait for a dip back to $4,490–$4,495 support before adding longs; buying into a +1.4% rally into tomorrow's NFP is late-cycle risk. If NFP disappoints, look for secondary entry near $4,475 on any sharp dip.
